mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate at 5.25% represents restrictive monetary policy. Sustained high rates compress equity valuations by raising discount rates on future cash flows. S&P 500 forward returns decline as the market reprices equities downward in response to persistent monetary tightening.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of observations when FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 4.5% signals tight labor market conditions. Sustained low unemployment pressures wage growth and unit labor costs, eroding corporate profit margins. Equity investors anticipate margin compression and reduce valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE remains below 4.7% for 2 consecutive months
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or correlation between UNRATE and subsequent equity returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts at 1,518 thousand units represent elevated construction activity. When combined with retail sales momentum (RSAFS at 372,034 million), strong demand signals suggest the economy is overheating. Equity markets anticipate Federal Reserve tightening response and price in lower future growth, producing negative 20-day returns.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,518 and RSAFS at 372,034 as of March 2007
regime_hint: late-cycle demand surge with overheating risk
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the joint signal (HOUST AND RSAFS) shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production at 112.49 (2017=100) reflects current manufacturing capacity utilization. Declining INDPRO signals weakening supply-side momentum and potential demand destruction. Equity investors interpret supply weakness as a leading indicator of recession, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when INDPRO declines, or Rank IC between INDPRO momentum and forward returns is below 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment at 137,684 thousand represents strong labor market. When payroll growth decelerates month-over-month below 100 thousand, it signals weakening labor demand and potential economic slowdown. Equity markets reprice downward in anticipation of earnings pressure from reduced consumer spending, producing negative 5-day returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: CPI inflation at 205.999 (1982-1984=100) reflects cumulative price pressures. When CPI growth accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year, the Federal Reserve maintains or increases restrictive policy stance. Equity investors anticipate sustained high real discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of high-inflation periods, or correlation between CPI growth and subsequent equity returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1 at 11,549.1 billion chained 2017 dollars) combined with housing starts decline signals demand deceleration. When HOUST falls below 1,400 thousand while GDPC1 growth slows below 2% annualized, the market reprices equities downward anticipating recession, producing negative 60-day returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 1,400 AND GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth < 2.0%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11,549.1 as of Q1 2007, HOUST at 1,518 as of March 2007
regime_hint: demand-side recession risk in late cycle
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the joint signal shows Rank IC < 0.06 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production weakness (INDPRO at 112.49) combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE at 4.5% rising) signals stagflationary pressure. When INDPRO declines while UNRATE rises simultaneously, equity investors face conflicting signals: margin pressure from weak supply meets demand destruction from labor market softening. The net effect is negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as uncertainty premium widens.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth < -0.1% AND UNRATE month-over-month change > 0.1pp
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 112.49 and UNRATE at 4.5% as of March-April 2007
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 58% of triggered instances, or the interaction signal shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 18-month rolling window.
